description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: U.S. Army facility at Rockford, Illinois
description_wiki: Camp Grant was a U.S. Army facility located in the southern outskirts of Rockford, Illinois, named in honor of American Civil War general Ulysses S. Grant.

description: former U.S. Army training camp during the World War I and World War II eras near Macon, Georgia
description_wiki: Camp Wheeler was a United States Army base near Macon, Georgia.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

aliases: Territory of New Caledonia and Dependencies
description: French special collectivity and archipelago in Oceania
description_wiki: New Caledonia is a French territory comprising a group of islands in the southwest Pacific Ocean, 220 km (140 mi) southwest of Vanuatu and 1,210 km (750 mi) east of Australia.

surface_forms: 31st Division that I was sent to
description: 1917-1968 United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 31st Chemical Brigade is an Army National Guard CBRN brigade.

description: comprises the Ohio Army National Guard and the Ohio Air National Guard
description_wiki: The Ohio National Guard comprises the Ohio Army National Guard and the Ohio Air National Guard.

description: island of the Solomon Islands chain in the South Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: Guadalcanal (; indigenous name: Isatabu) is the principal island in Guadalcanal Province of Solomon Islands, located in the southwestern Pacific Ocean, northeast of Australia.

description: 1942-1943 series of battles fought on and around Guadalcanal as part of World War II
island in Papua New Guinea
description_wiki: Bougainville Island ( ,  ; Tok Pisin: Bogenvil) is the main island of the Autonomous Region of Bougainville, which is part of Papua New Guinea.

description: island sovereign state in Oceania
description_wiki: Solomon Islands, also known simply as the Solomons, is an archipelagic country consisting of six major islands and over 1,000 smaller islands in Melanesia, Oceania, to the north-east of Australia.

description: United States Armed Forces decoration award
description_wiki: The Bronze Star Medal (BSM) is a United States Armed Forces decoration awarded to members of the United States Armed Forces for either heroic achievement, heroic service, meritorious achievement, or meritorious service in a combat zone.

description: hill on Bougainville, WW2 battlefield, Papua New Guinea
American broadcast journalist and author (born 1940)
description_wiki: Thomas John Brokaw (; born February 6, 1940) is an American author and retired network television journalist.

aliases: Gulf War 2/battle of Ramadi/battle of Faluya
description: 2003–2011 war after an American-led invasion
description_wiki: The Iraq War (Arabic: حرب العراق, romanized: ḥarb al-ʿirāq), also referred to as the Second Gulf War, was a protracted armed conflict in Iraq from 2003 to 2011.

description: archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

description: largest island of the Philippines
description_wiki: Luzon ( loo-ZON, Tagalog: [luˈson]) is the largest and most populous island in the Philippines.

description: extension of the South China Sea on Luzon in the Philippines
description_wiki: Lingayen Gulf is a large gulf on northwestern Luzon in the Philippines, stretching 56 km (35 mi).

description: battle in 1945 during World War II fought between Japan and the United States with the Commonwealth of the Philippines
description_wiki: The Battle of Manila (3 February – 3 March  1945) was a major battle during the Philippine campaign of 1944–45, during the Second World War.

aliases: Queen City of the Pacific and others
description: capital city of the Philippines
description_wiki: Manila, officially the City of Manila, is the capital and second-most populous city of the Philippines after Quezon City.

description: city of the Philippines
description_wiki: Baguio (UK:  BAG-ee-oh, US:  BAH-ghee-oh, -⁠OH, Tagalog: [ˈbaɡjo]), officially the City of Baguio (Ibaloi: Siudad ne Bagiw; Ilocano: Siudad ti Baguio; Tagalog: Lungsod ng Baguio), is a  highly urbanized city in the Cordillera Administrative Region, Philippines.

description: mosquito-borne infectious disease
description_wiki: Malaria is a mosquito-borne infectious disease that is transmitted by the bite of Anopheles mosquitoes.

description: scientific article published on 01 December 1952
bombardment of Hiroshima in Japan carried out by the United States using an atomic bomb at the end of the Second World War on 6 August 1945
(de facto) national library of the United States of America
description_wiki: The Library of Congress (LC or sometimes LOC) is a research library in Washington, D.C., serving as the library and research service for the United States Congress and the de facto national library of the United States.

description: national university of the Philippines
description_wiki: The University of the Philippines (UP; Filipino: Unibersidad ng Pilipinas or Pamantasan ng Pilipinas) is a state public university system and the national university of the Philippines under Republic Act No.
